proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with npm1_mutation=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with npm1_mutation=0.
Patients with tp53_mutation=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with tp53_mutation=0.
Patients with complex_karyotype=1 have a lower objective_response rate than patients with complex_karyotype=0.
Patients with unfit_for_intensive=1 have a higher objective_response rate than patients with unfit_for_intensive=0 (paradoxical association in this cohort).

analyses.result_summary: Response rate 23.5% in npm1+ vs 16.7% in npm1-. Absolute difference +6.7 percentage points; chi-square p=5.8e-70.
Response rate 16.6% in tp53+ vs 19.0% in tp53-. Difference -2.4 pp; chi-square p=3.5e-5.
Response rate 16.8% in CK+ vs 19.1% in CK-. Difference -2.3 pp; chi-square p=3.3e-6.

Response rate 22.1% in unfit vs 16.5% in fit. Difference +5.6 pp; chi-square p=1.9e-55. Direction is opposite to the usual clinical expectation that unfit patients have lower responses; suggests an interaction with treatment assignment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher blast_pct_marrow is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a higher objective_response rate.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a lower objective_response rate.
Higher wbc_k_per_ul is associated with a lower objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Mean ECOG 0.67 in responders vs 0.83 in non-responders. Mean diff -0.157; Welch t-test p=2.3e-95.
Mean blast% 58.5 in responders vs 60.3 in non-responders. Mean diff -1.80; p=2.0e-16.
Mean albumin 3.82 in responders vs 3.80 in non-responders. Mean diff +0.029; p=2.7e-7.
Mean weight loss 3.44% in responders vs 3.95% in non-responders. Mean diff -0.51 pp; p=1.7e-33.
Mean CRP 5.69 in responders vs 6.17 in non-responders. Mean diff -0.48; p=1.6e-6.
Mean WBC 19.3 in responders vs 20.4 in non-responders. Mean diff -1.11; p=1.5e-4.

proposed_hypotheses.text: age_years is associated with objective_response rate.
sex_female is associated with objective_response rate.
secondary_aml is associated with objective_response rate.
flt3_itd is associated with objective_response rate.
idh1_mutation or idh2_mutation is associated with objective_response rate.

analyses.result_summary: Mean age 67.98 in responders vs 67.99 in non-responders. Diff -0.018; p=0.88. No marginal association.
Response 18.9% in females vs 18.6% in males. Diff +0.003; p=0.42.
Response 18.8% in secondary AML vs 18.7% otherwise. Diff +0.001; p=0.85.
Response 18.9% with flt3_itd vs 18.7% without. Diff +0.002; p=0.64.
idh1+ 19.1% vs 18.7% (p=0.60); idh2+ 19.1% vs 18.7% (p=0.47). No marginal effect on response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine is associated with higher objective_response (positive main effect).
treatment_7plus3 is associated with higher objective_response (main effect).
treatment_midostaurin is associated with higher objective_response (main effect).
treatment_gilteritinib is associated with higher objective_response (main effect).
treatment_ivosidenib is associated with higher objective_response (main effect).
treatment_enasidenib is associated with higher objective_response (main effect).

analyses.result_summary: Response 22.0% on venetoclax+azacitidine vs 16.6% off. Diff +5.4 pp; chi-square p=6.6e-51. Strong positive marginal association.
Response 18.9% on 7+3 vs 18.6% off. Diff +0.003; p=0.46. No detectable main effect.
Response 18.6% on midostaurin vs 18.8% off. Diff -0.002; p=0.67. No main effect.
Response 18.1% on gilteritinib vs 18.9% off. Diff -0.007; p=0.14. No main effect.
Response 18.8% on ivosidenib vs 18.7% off. Diff +0.001; p=0.93. No main effect.
Response 18.6% on enasidenib vs 18.8% off. Diff -0.002; p=0.79. No main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable logistic adjustment for all 35 features and treatments, the strongest predictors of objective_response are ecog_ps (negative), npm1_mutation (positive), unfit_for_intensive (positive), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine (positive), weight_loss_pct_6mo (negative), blast_pct_marrow (negative), albumin_g_dl (positive), complex_karyotype (negative), and tp53_mutation (negative).

analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logistic regression confirms: ecog_ps coef=-0.36 (p=9e-93), npm1_mutation coef=+0.44 (p=2e-71), unfit_for_intensive coef=+0.37 (p=2e-57), treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine coef=+0.36 (p=8e-54), weight_loss_pct_6mo coef=-0.037 (p=3e-31), blast_pct_marrow coef=-0.005 (p=1e-17), albumin_g_dl coef=+0.12 (p=1e-7), complex_karyotype coef=-0.17 (p=6e-7), tp53_mutation coef=-0.19 (p=4e-6). Notably, treatment_midostaurin/gilteritinib/ivosidenib/enasidenib/7+3 each have coefficients near zero (|coef|<0.05) and p>0.1 after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_midostaurin improves objective_response in patients with flt3_itd=1 (the canonical clinical indication: FLT3 inhibitor in FLT3-mutated AML).
treatment_gilteritinib improves objective_response in patients with flt3_itd=1.

analyses.code: logit(y ~ tx + flt3_itd + tx*flt3_itd) interaction term
analyses.result_summary: Within flt3_itd=1 (n=10000): midostaurin response 17.6% (n=1468) vs 19.1% off (n=8532); diff -1.5 pp, chi-square p=0.19. tx*flt3_itd interaction coef=-0.106, p=0.20. No detectable benefit; trend is mildly negative, contradicting the canonical clinical hypothesis.

Within flt3_itd=1 (n=10000): gilteritinib response 19.1% (n=1491) vs 18.9% off (n=8509); diff +0.2 pp, p=0.86. tx*flt3_itd interaction coef=+0.079, p=0.32. No detectable benefit in FLT3-ITD+ patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_ivosidenib improves objective_response in patients with idh1_mutation=1.
treatment_enasidenib improves objective_response in patients with idh2_mutation=1.

analyses.result_summary: Within idh1_mutation=1 (n=3624): ivosidenib response 15.4% (n=298) vs 19.4% off (n=3326); diff -4.0 pp, p=0.11. tx*idh1 interaction coef=-0.304, p=0.077. Actually trends toward worse response in IDH1-mutated patients on ivosidenib (against expectation).
Within idh2_mutation=1 (n=4679): enasidenib response 19.5% (n=354) vs 19.1% off (n=4325); diff +0.4 pp, p=0.92. tx*idh2 interaction coef=+0.039, p=0.79. No benefit in IDH2-mutated patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: None of the four targeted agents (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib) shows a positive interaction with its canonical target mutation; therefore none of them produces a clinically meaningful improvement in objective_response in this cohort, even within mutation-positive subgroups.

analyses.code: Joint screen of tx*mutation interactions across all four targeted agents x [flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1, idh2, npm1, tp53, ck, secondary_aml, unfit_for_intensive]
analyses.result_summary: Across 36 targeted-tx-by-mutation interaction tests, no interaction reached p<0.001. Largest positive observation was treatment_enasidenib*sex_female coef=+0.314 p=2.2e-4 (clinically implausible target mutation-independent effect). All four targeted agents fail to demonstrate response benefit in their canonical biomarker-defined subpopulation. Best estimate of the average targeted-agent effect on response within target-mutation+ subgroup is +/- 0-4 pp, none statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a substantially larger positive effect on objective_response in patients with npm1_mutation=1 than in patients with npm1_mutation=0 (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: tx*npm1_mutation interaction coefficient = +1.055 (p=5.5e-102) in logistic model with main effects. In npm1+ patients (n=14990), ven/aza response 26.6% vs 16.0% off (diff +10.6 pp). In npm1- patients, ven/aza shows essentially no benefit. Strong positive interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine has a substantially larger positive effect on objective_response in patients with unfit_for_intensive=1 than in fit patients (positive interaction).

analyses.result_summary: tx*unfit_for_intensive interaction coefficient = +0.883 (p=1.1e-78). Within unfit (n=20056), ven/aza response 30.0% vs 16.5% off; within fit (n=29944), ven/aza 16.2% vs 17.0% off. Both unfit and npm1+ are positive modifiers.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is reduced (or eliminated) in patients with tp53_mutation=1 compared to tp53_mutation=0 (negative interaction).
treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine effect on objective_response is reduced (or eliminated) in patients with complex_karyotype=1 compared to complex_karyotype=0 (negative interaction).

analyses.result_summary: tx*tp53_mutation interaction coefficient = -0.409 (p=4.7e-7). Within tp53+ patients (n=4985), ven/aza 16.4% vs 16.7% off (no benefit). Within tp53- patients (n=45015), ven/aza 22.6% vs 16.6% off (diff +6.0 pp). TP53 mutation abrogates the ven/aza response benefit.

tx*complex_karyotype interaction coefficient = -0.306 (p=6.0e-6). Within CK+ patients (n=7462), ven/aza 17.5% vs 16.3% off (diff +1.2 pp, ns). Within CK- patients (n=42538), ven/aza 22.7% vs 16.7% off (diff +6.1 pp, p=3e-55). Complex karyotype substantially attenuates the ven/aza response benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a markedly higher objective_response specifically in patients who are simultaneously unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1; in patients lacking either feature, the effect is small or absent.

analyses.code: Stratified 2x2 by (unfit, npm1): response on ven/aza vs off
analyses.result_summary: Stratified by unfit x npm1: unfit=0/npm1=0: ven/aza 16.2% vs 17.2% off (diff -1.0 pp); unfit=0/npm1=1: 15.7% vs 16.0% (diff -0.3 pp); unfit=1/npm1=0: 16.9% vs 16.4% (diff +0.5 pp); unfit=1/npm1=1: 63.3% (n=2401) vs 16.0% (n=3585) (diff +47.4 pp, chi-square p=6.7e-310). The treatment effect is essentially confined to the doubly-positive subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Including a 3-way interaction term tx*unfit*npm1 in a logistic regression yields a large, highly significant positive coefficient, while the 2-way tx*unfit and tx*npm1 main interaction terms become small and non-significant — confirming that the response benefit requires both factors simultaneously.

analyses.code: Logit y ~ tx + unfit + npm1 + tp53 + ck + tx*unfit + tx*npm1 + tx*tp53 + tx*ck + tx*unfit*npm1
analyses.result_summary: tx*unfit*npm1 coef = +2.206 (p=8.1e-176); tx*unfit alone coef +0.091 (p=0.11); tx*npm1 alone coef +0.027 (p=0.68); tx*tp53 coef -0.534 (p=4.2e-10); tx*complex_karyotype coef -0.379 (p=8.7e-8). Confirms response benefit requires unfit AND npm1+ jointly, and is suppressed by tp53+ and complex_karyotype+.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine produces a very large objective_response benefit (~60 percentage points absolute) specifically in patients who satisfy ALL of: unfit_for_intensive=1, npm1_mutation=1, tp53_mutation=0, and complex_karyotype=0.

analyses.code: Stratified 2x2 in mask = unfit=1 & npm1=1 & tp53=0 & ck=0 vs complement
analyses.result_summary: Within unfit & npm1+ & tp53- & ck- (n=4531): ven/aza response 78.6% (1407/1790) vs 15.8% (433/2741) off; absolute diff +62.8 pp, chi-square p essentially 0. Outside this subgroup (n=45469): ven/aza response 16.4% (2989/18230) vs 16.7% (4548/27239) off; diff -0.3 pp, chi-square p=0.42. The entire ven/aza treatment effect is concentrated in this 4-feature-defined subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable subgroup, no continuous laboratory feature (age, WBC, blast %, albumin, LDH, weight loss, CRP, NLR, etc.) materially modifies the ven/aza response benefit beyond the four binary modifiers already identified.

analyses.code: Logit interaction tx*standardized continuous feature, screened across all 18 continuous variables
analyses.result_summary: Across 18 continuous*tx interaction tests, the largest standardized coefficients were tx*blast_pct_marrow +0.068 (p=0.0035), tx*weight_loss +0.050 (p=0.039), tx*bun -0.046 (p=0.047). All small (|coef|<0.07) and at most marginally significant; none would meaningfully refine the subgroup definition. Conclude that the four binary modifiers (unfit, npm1, tp53, complex_karyotype) capture essentially all heterogeneity.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 has a negative interaction with complex_karyotype: in CK+ patients, 7+3 reduces objective_response compared to no 7+3.

analyses.result_summary: tx*complex_karyotype interaction coefficient for 7+3 = -0.220 (p=1.1e-3). Within CK+ patients (n=7462): 7+3 response 15.5% (n=3415) vs 17.9% off (n=4047); diff -2.3 pp, chi-square p=0.008. Within CK- patients: 7+3 19.5% vs 18.8%, diff +0.7 pp, p=0.06. Modest negative interaction; 7+3 may slightly hurt response in CK+ AML.

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_7plus3 improves objective_response in fit patients (unfit_for_intensive=0) compared to no 7+3.

analyses.result_summary: In fit patients (n=29944): 7+3 response 16.7% (n=13474) vs 16.3% off (n=16470); diff +0.4 pp, p=0.38. In unfit patients (n=20056): 7+3 response 22.1% (n=9106) vs 22.1% off (n=10950); diff 0.0 pp, p=0.99. 7+3 has no detectable response benefit even in fit patients in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Exhaustive 2-binary-feature stratified subgroup search across all six treatments will reveal the venetoclax_azacitidine effect in unfit AND npm1+ patients as by far the largest treatment effect, with no other treatment showing a comparable subgroup effect.

analyses.code: Loop over all (treatment, feat1, val1, feat2, val2) with subgroup n>=200 and n_per_arm>=50; rank by |z| of treatment-effect difference
analyses.result_summary: Top subgroup by |z| across all six treatments: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine in unfit_for_intensive=1 & npm1_mutation=1 (z=40.88, diff=+47.4 pp). Next top venetoclax subgroups all confirm the npm1+/unfit theme. For midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3 the largest subgroup |z| values are <3.5, with absolute response differences <12 pp and unstable / opposite to expected directions. Confirms ven/aza in unfit+npm1+ as the unique large heterogeneous treatment effect in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Exhaustive 3-binary-feature subgroup search for venetoclax_azacitidine will identify unfit_for_intensive=1 & complex_karyotype=0 & npm1_mutation=1 (or equivalently with tp53_mutation=0) as the highest-effect subgroup.

analyses.code: Exhaustive search over 3 binary features, n>=300, both arms>=50
analyses.result_summary: Top 3-feature subgroups for venetoclax_azacitidine: (1) unfit=1 & ck=0 & npm1=1: tx 71.7% (n=2025) vs 16.1% off (n=3052), diff +55.6 pp, |z|=46.2; (2) unfit=1 & npm1=1 & tp53=0: tx 69.1% vs 15.7% off, diff +53.4 pp, |z|=44.9; (3) unfit=1 & idh2=0 & npm1=1: diff +48.2 pp, |z|=39.5. The negative modifiers tp53 and complex_karyotype each independently lift the within-subgroup response when removed.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Combining all four key modifiers (unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0) yields an even larger ven/aza response benefit than any 3-feature subgroup.

analyses.code: mask = unfit==1 & npm1==1 & tp53==0 & ck==0; cross-tab tx vs y
analyses.result_summary: Within full 4-feature subgroup (n=4531): venetoclax_azacitidine response 78.6% (1407/1790) vs 15.8% (433/2741) off treatment; absolute diff +62.8 pp. This exceeds any 3-feature subgroup. Both negative modifiers (tp53+, ck+) act as suppressors of the favorable effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding a fifth binary feature (sex, secondary_aml, flt3_itd, flt3_tkd, idh1, idh2) to the 4-feature ven/aza subgroup does NOT meaningfully refine the treatment effect — i.e., the four-feature definition is parsimonious and complete among the binary modifiers.

analyses.code: For each candidate 5th feature, restrict and recompute response on/off ven/aza
analyses.result_summary: Within unfit & npm1+ & tp53- & ck-, partitioning by sex_female yields tx-vs-control diffs of +61.7 pp (male) and +64.0 pp (female); by secondary_aml +62.8/+62.7; by flt3_itd +63.6/+59.6; by flt3_tkd +62.7/+64.9; by idh1 +63.2/+57.7; by idh2 +63.7/+55.0. All within ~5-7 pp of each other; no fifth binary feature substantively modifies the effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the favorable ven/aza subgroup, additional targeted treatments (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) do NOT further improve response on top of ven/aza.

analyses.code: Within unfit & npm1+, condition on each other treatment
analyses.result_summary: Within unfit & npm1+, ven/aza response stratified by each co-treatment: with/without midostaurin: 66.5%/62.8% vs 16.5%/15.9% — absolute treatment effects 50.0 vs 47.0 pp (similar). Same pattern for gilteritinib (61.9/63.6), ivosidenib (63.8/63.3), enasidenib (63.1/63.3), 7+3 (62.5/64.0). No co-treatment systematically modifies the ven/aza effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Aside from venetoclax_azacitidine, no other treatment in the cohort produces a clinically meaningful (>10 pp) response benefit in any biomarker- or clinical-feature-defined subgroup with adequate sample size.

analyses.code: Sweep over (tx in {midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3}) x (1- and 2-feature subgroups) and rank by absolute treatment effect
analyses.result_summary: Across all five non-ven/aza treatments and all 1- and 2-binary-feature subgroups (n>=300), the largest absolute response differences observed were: enasidenib in flt3_itd=1 & idh1_mutation=1 +11.0 pp (n=53/668, |z|=1.7, ns); ivosidenib in idh2+ & secondary_aml +/-11.9 pp (negative effect, |z|=3.5, n=79). All other treatment-by-subgroup effects are <10 pp and not consistent in direction. There is no second clinically meaningful treatment effect in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: For each of the five non-ven/aza treatments, a continuous-feature interaction screen will not reveal a clinically meaningful response-modifying subgroup.

analyses.code: Logit y ~ tx + cont + tx*cont (cont standardized) for each tx and each of 18 continuous features
analyses.result_summary: Across 5 (non-ven/aza) treatments x 18 continuous features = 90 interaction tests, the largest |coef| was enasidenib*hemoglobin_g_dl -0.140 (p=0.0012) and gilteritinib*alkaline_phosphatase +0.101 (p=0.0014). Even nominally significant findings have small standardized coefficients that translate to very modest changes in absolute response across plausible feature ranges. None defines a clinically meaningful treatment-effect subgroup. Confirms the only large heterogeneous effect is ven/aza in unfit+npm1+ patients without tp53 or complex_karyotype.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT HETEROGENEITY HYPOTHESIS: treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine increases objective_response by approximately +63 percentage points (78.6% vs 15.8%) specifically in the subgroup defined by unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0; outside this subgroup the average treatment effect is essentially zero. The unfavorable suppressors (tp53_mutation=1 and complex_karyotype=1) each independently abrogate the benefit.

FINAL: No other treatment (midostaurin, gilteritinib, ivosidenib, enasidenib, 7+3) shows a clinically meaningful, statistically robust positive effect on objective_response in this cohort, either as a main effect or in any reasonably-sized subgroup defined by mutations, performance status, fitness, or laboratory features.

analyses.code: Confirmation: cross-tab tx vs y restricted to unfit==1 & npm1==1 & tp53==0 & ck==0 vs complement
analyses.result_summary: FINAL subgroup confirmation: in (unfit_for_intensive=1 AND npm1_mutation=1 AND tp53_mutation=0 AND complex_karyotype=0) (n=4531): venetoclax_azacitidine response 78.6% (1407/1790) vs 15.8% off (433/2741); absolute diff +62.8 pp; chi-square p essentially 0. In the complement (n=45469): ven/aza 16.4% vs 16.7% off, diff -0.3 pp, p=0.42. The treatment effect is fully concentrated in the four-feature subgroup; the two suppressors (tp53+, complex_karyotype+) are required components of the subgroup definition.

FINAL no-effect confirmation for other treatments: in their canonical biomarker subgroups, midostaurin (FLT3-ITD+): -1.5 pp (p=0.19); gilteritinib (FLT3-ITD+): +0.2 pp (p=0.86); ivosidenib (IDH1+): -4.0 pp (p=0.11); enasidenib (IDH2+): +0.4 pp (p=0.92); 7+3 (fit): +0.4 pp (p=0.38). All near null and non-significant. No second meaningful treatment-effect subgroup was identified across exhaustive 1-, 2-, and 3-feature binary subgroup searches and continuous interaction screens.
